description:
An aerodynamics engineer has provided a 3D-scanned STL mesh of a UAV wing, located at /home/ga/Desktop/scanned_uav_wing.stl. Your task is to reverse-engineer this 'dead' mesh into a fully parametric OpenVSP model.

1. In OpenVSP, import the STL file (File > Import > STL).
2. Add a new standard Wing component (Geom > Wing).
3. Adjust the new Wing component's parameters so its 3D outline closely matches the imported mesh. You must match: Total Span, Root Chord, Tip Chord, Sweep, and Dihedral.
4. Save the reconstructed model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/reconstructed_wing.vsp3.

The final saved .vsp3 model must contain BOTH the original MeshGeom (the imported STL) and your new WingGeom aligned to it.
